qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Attacker-controlled SSH options enable local command execution via ProxyCommand
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is a workspace authorization bypass in `packages/mcp/src/tools/knowledge.ts` and `packages/mcp/src/tool-scoping.ts`; this finding is SSH option injection in `packages/server/src/adapters/ssh.ts` and does not involve `knowledge_search`, `knowledge_get_node`, or missing workspace scoping.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's missing workspace-scoping checks in the MCP knowledge tool handlers.
evidence: The advisory describes cross-workspace data leakage caused by `knowledge_search` and `knowledge_get_node` in `packages/mcp/src/tools/knowledge.ts` not receiving `authContext` and not enforcing scoped `workspaceId` handling.

The only final qualifying finding in this run targets `packages/server/src/adapters/ssh.ts` and claims local command execution through attacker-controlled `sshOptions.ProxyCommand` during environment provisioning.

Because the finding concerns a different package (`server` vs `mcp`), different entrypoints (environment provisioning vs MCP knowledge tools), and a different root cause (command execution via raw SSH options vs missing authorization checks), it is an open-world finding rather than an advisory match.
